observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:





Exhaust all possible efforts to identify the persons to whom the LGU has money obligations and send written notices to the concerned suppliers and creditors to inform them on their unpaid claims recorded in the books of the LGU totaling to P 2,899,265.11, to establish the validity, existence, and correctness of the recorded payable accounts.


Upon determination that the concerned suppliers/contractors are no longer interested in their respective claims, to instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entries to revert back to the unappropriated surplus the long outstanding payables and correct the other above noted misstatements, and moving forward, ensure the proper recording of financial transactions in the books, for the fair presentation of the Other Payables and other affected accounts in the financial statements; and

To instruct the Municipal Accountant to record the necessary adjusting entries and moving forward, ensure the proper recording of financial transactions in the books, for the fair presentation of the Other Payables and other affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.reason: Management has yet to take full corrective action, as they are still in the process of reconciling accounts and verifying transactions that require adjustments.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:

Conduct a detailed review of the transactions related to the accounts with existing negative balances to identify the nature of such abnormal balances, and the necessary and appropriate actions to be taken; and

Prior to the posting of transaction, regularly reconcile the outstanding balances of the payable, receivable or cash advances to the amount of payment, collection or liquidation; all to ensure fair presentation of the accounts balances presented in the financial statements.
observations.reason: Management has yet to take full corrective action, as they are still in the process of reconciling accounts and verifying transactions that require adjustments.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the management to:


Identify the nature and cause/s of the unreconciled balance totaling P 2,458,611.73 and to have a reconciliation with the concerned agencies to establish the actual amounts of unpaid taxes and/or compulsory contributions.


Moving forward, to have a regular reconciliation of the balances of Inter-Agency Payable accounts to the actual remittances to settle immediately any identified unremitted amounts and facilitate the preparation of the necessary adjusting entries to present fairly the balances of Due to BIR, Due to GSIS, Due to Pag-IBIG and Due to PhilHealth accounts in the financial statements; and


To include in the budget for the succeeding year an amount sufficient and intended to settle unpaid taxes/contributions from previous years, when necessary, to fund any corresponding government share of mandatory contributions of employees.
observations.managementAction: Management remitted the full amount withheld and due for the year 2024 within the prescribed period, except for certain payables that required subsequent funding to cover the additional government share of employees’ mandatory contributions.

observations.recommendation:

Exert best effort to locate pertinent documents to substantiate the dormant accounts and assess collectability of the dormant accounts;

If collection is no longer feasible, undertake the appropriate actions for the proper disposition of these accounts, following the procedures outlined in COA Circular 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023; and

Consider the recognition of impairment losses for those dormant accounts with no potential collection or recovery pursuant to Item 7.3 of COA Circular No.  2016-005, all to ensure fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management to:

Revert to the unappropriated surplus of GF the unexpended balances from the completed projects funded by 20% DF totaling to P 1,449,838.94 in compliance with pertinent rules; and

After the reversion, identify the PPAs to which the unexpended balances may be utilized and to authorize the utilization of such fund through the issuance of a subsequent enactment.
observations.reason: Management has not implemented the recommendations, as the unexpended balances from completed 20% DF projects have not yet been reverted to the unappropriated surplus, nor has a subsequent enactment been issued to authorize their utilization for new PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to instruct the: (a)  Municipal Accountant to: (i) prepare adjusting journal entries to reclassify the subject PPE items and Investment Property, Building account to their appropriate specific PPE accounts, and recognize the corresponding accumulated depreciation accounts; and (b) observed the capitalization threshold provided under COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, in order to ensure transparency of the accounts presented and the fair presentation of the account balances in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Management, through the Municipal Accountant, record the completed projects in the appropriate PPE account and provide the corresponding depreciation expense for the year 2024, in compliance with Paragraph 25 of IPSAS 1 and the COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Management, through the Municipal Accountant, prepare adjusting journal entries to correctly recognize the completed asset, exclude the unrelated labor cost and provide the correct accumulated depreciation and depreciation expense for the year 2024 to come up with the adjusted net book value of the assets in compliance with Paragraph 14 of IPSAS 17 and Section 50 of NGAS Manual for LGUs for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statement.

We recommended the Management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to recompute the depreciation of Road Networks in compliance with Item No. 14, Section IV of COA Circular No. 2015-008, by not providing salvage value to the respective assets and effect the necessary adjusting entry to accurately restate the net book value of Road Networks.

We recommended the Management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to: (a) prepare adjusting journal entries to correct the classification of the accounts and to present fairly the balance of Advances to Officers and Employees, Other Receivable and Due to Officers and Employees accounts in the financial statements; (b) record all cash advances for travelling and training expenses to Advances to Officers and Employees account and that the liquidations, refunds, or reimbursements are appropriately recorded to the same account and/or the relevant liability account, as applicable; and (c) regularly monitor the subsidiary ledgers for any abnormal balances, investigate discrepancies and take appropriate action to establish accurate and reliable account balances in the financial statements in line with Section 112 of PD 1445 and Paragraph 25 of IPSAS I.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to facilitate fair presentation of the accounts presented in the financial statements, by: a. reviewing items currently recorded under Other Receivables, determine their proper classification, and prepare adjusting journal entries (AJE) to reclassify mis-posted items to their corresponding accounts in accordance with COA Circular 2015-009; b. regularly reconciling the outstanding balances of the receivables or cash advances to the amount of payment, collection or liquidation to establish the correctness and reliability of the accounts; and c. undertaking appropriate actions for the collection/liquidation of the dormant receivables.

We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. send confirmation letters to creditors to determine the existence and validity of AP and revert immediately to unappropriated surplus those undocumented/invalid/ non-existing payables without valid claims that is outstanding for more than two (2) years, in compliance with Section 98 of PD 1445; b. substantiate entries in the General Journal with adequate supporting documents to support the validity of the recorded liabilities in accordance with Paragraph 25 of IPSAS 1; c. record payables in the SL based on the date of recognition or incurrence of a liability, and establish a systematic process for aging accounts payable to effectively monitor and manage outstanding liabilities; and d. exert effort to analyze and investigate the cause of negative balances in the AP and make appropriate adjustments or require refund, in case of overpayment, or consider the possibility of reverting the amount to Accumulated Surplus/Deficit in case all efforts proved futile.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to facilitate fair presentation of balances presented in the financial statements in accordance with Paragraph 25 of IPSAS 1 by: a. analyzing the related transactions and investigating further the noted condition and recording the adjusting journal entries, as necessary; and b. maintaining a separate subsidiary ledger for each special account of the LGU.

We recommended that the Management require the: (a) municipal Treasurer, to facilitate the immediate release of the amounts due to the concerned barangays to strictly adhere with Section 271 of RA 7160 and undertake necessary actions that would ensure the regular release of such funds to the concerned Barangay for them to utilize for development programs; and (b) municipal Accountant, to conduct a thorough reconciliation of the balances under the Due to LGUs account, identify items that are already settled, and adjust the records accordingly to facilitate fair presentation of accounts presented in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Management, through the Municipal Accountant, to: a. prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to accurately record the utilization of fund transfers and reclassify to income the unused balances of completed projects that no longer require return to the SA in accordance with Paragraph 25 of IPSAS I for fair presentation of Due to NGAs account; b. consider the applicability of COA Circular No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023, which amends COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 subject to submission of required schedules and documents; c. return the unused balance of fund transfers to SA upon completion of the project in accordance with Item 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994; and d. enhance coordination with implementing offices to ensure that available funds are identified and utilized promptly.

We recommended that the Management, thru the Municipal Accountant to: a. reclassify the unexpended balance of LDRRM Fund from the GF to the TF books under “Trust Liability – DRRMF” account to ensure proper recoding of financial transactions in compliance with Item 5.1.10 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012; b. prepare adjusting journal entries to present fairly the balance of Trust Liability – DRRMF, Transfer of Unspent Current Year DRRM Funds to the Trust Funds, and Prior Period Adjustment in the financial statements in line with Annex C of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and the provisions of the Revised Chart of Accounts prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-009; and c. reconcile the Cash in Bank, Local Currency Current Account (STF) with the Trust Liability – DRRMF to ensure that the unexpended balance of LDRRM Fund is fully back up by cash to comply with the requirement of RA No. 10121 and the related regulations.

We recommended that the Management enhance its financial reporting and ensure that: a. budget allocation for specific expense account is carefully studied and projected based on historical data and anticipated needs during budget preparation to have a reliable measurement base for fund utilization; b. all transactions are properly classified and recorded in the specific or appropriate accounts and use, for fair presentation of account balances in the financial statements, in accordance with Paragraph 25 of IPSAS 1 and COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015; and c. regularly compare budget utilization reports against actual expenditures to quickly detect and correct any misclassification or improper budget allocation.

We recommended that the Management maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund by accelerating the implementation of pending projects, identifying the root causes of non-implementation, and developing practical, targeted solutions to ensure the effective execution of developmental initiatives.

We recommended that Management review the unexpended balances from previous appropriations and ensure proper monitoring of completed projects funded under the 20% DF and CO as part of the annual budget process to properly monitor budget allocation and utilization.

Furthermore, we recommended that Management initiate the immediate reversion of savings for reallocation to optimize resource utilization and to utilize the identified savings to other development programs of the LGU.

We recommended that the Management strictly adhere to the guidelines set forth under COA Circular No. 2012-002 and NDRRMC DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1, and refrain from charging against the LDRRM Fund those PPAs not pertaining to disaster-related activities and/or not in accordance with the approved budget for which the fund was established.

Moreover, we recommended that the Management require the MDRRMO to abreast himself of the rules and regulations governing the mandate of the Office and ensure proper coordination and install a review mechanism with the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to strengthen the LGU’s internal financial controls, thereby enhancing transparency and accountability in the utilization of LDRRM Fund.

We recommended that the Management, through the MDRRMO, prepare LDRRMFIP on the use and release of the Unexpended balance of the LDRRM Fund under the STF in compliance with Section 5.1.2 and 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 to minimize unauthorized or improper use of the fund.

We recommended that the Management cease from the practice of immediately issuing the Certificate of Final Acceptance prior to the lapse of the defect’s liability period or one year from the project completion date to ensure possible recourse for the LGU in cases of uncorrected defects and to protect Government’s resources and property against loss or wastage, in compliance with 62.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that the Management, thru the Municipal Accountant, require the contractor to post warranty security before processing of final payment, pursuant to Section 62.2.3.3 of the Revised IRR of 9184 to safeguard the interests of the LGU and/or general public in the use of the infrastructure projects of the government.

We recommended that the Management initiate the recovery of the amount disbursed as financial assistance to a religious institution, as the transaction lacks legal basis and is considered irregular under applicable laws and regulations.

Furthermore, we recommended that the Management, moving forward, exercise diligence and prudence in the processing and approval of disbursements, ensuring that public funds are granted only for lawful and clearly defined public purpose, in line with Section 29(2), Article VI of the 1987 Philippine Constitution and applicable COA rules and regulations.

We recommended the Management thru the Municipal Accountant to: (a) immediately submit the lacking DVs and its complete supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor; and (b) ensure compliance with relevant rules and regulations on the timely and complete submission of disbursement vouchers to established regularity, propriety and validity of the transactions and to enable the conduct of audit, review, evaluation, and assessment.

We recommended the Management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to: (a)  reclassify the subject PPE items and Investment Property, Building account to their appropriate specific PPE accounts, and recognize the corresponding accumulated depreciation accounts; and (b) observed the capitalization threshold provided under COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, in order to ensure transparency of the accounts presented and the fair presentation of the account balances in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already prepared the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the assets in their appropriate account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:





Require the delegated custodians of goods and supplies to submit the necessary reports showing the receipt, issuances and ending balances of the inventories under their custody to the GSO ;


Direct the GSO, with the assistance of the concerned officers of the LGU to: (a) prepare the SSMI weekly and submit the same to the Accounting Office to facilitate the recording of the issuances of goods and supplies under the proper expense accounts; and (b) conduct periodic physical inventory of supplies, including those under the custody of the MHO, MSWDO and Municipal Treasurer, to whom the keeping of some supplies and goods are delegated with and prepare the corresponding RPCI; and


Require the Municipal Accountant to: (a) prepare the necessary adjusting entries to present the Welfare Goods for Distribution, Accountable Forms, Plates and Stickers, Drugs and Medicines Inventory, Medical, Dental and Laboratory Supplies Inventory and the related expenses accounts in the financial statements; (b) maintain Supplies Ledger Cards for each item of the stocks; and (c) have a regular reconciliation and coordination with the GSO for the timely and accurate recording of the issuance and receipt of inventories.
observations.managementAction: The reports necessary to show the receipts, issuances and ending balances were duly submitted; in addition, the necessary journal entries were also posted and lastly, regular reconciliation of balances have been implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to reevaluate the recording of the utilization of funds received from NGAs, to record the necessary adjusting entries and, moving forward, to ensure the proper recording of financial transactions in the books, particularly on the receipts, utilizations and liquidations of fund transfers, for the fair presentation of the Due to NGAs account in the financial statements.

We recommended that the management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to:

Strictly enforce that all collections are turned over to the Liquidating Officer on a daily basis, in compliance with Section 60 (A) of the Local Treasury Operations Manual.

Ensure that the official receipts were properly included and filed on the month of collection for proper recording of collections in accordance with paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1; and 

Notify the taxpayers of the cut-off time being set for the collection process and encourage them to pay within such period to facilitate immediate deposit of all collections and to properly manage the risk of loss or misappropriation of funds, and misstatements of related accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer already complied with the audit recommendations

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant comply with the preparation of the reconciliation of actual amounts on a comparable basis as presented in the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts and in the Statement of Financial Performance for the year ended to provide clarity and understanding on the difference that may be noted between the components of the General Purpose Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant complied with the preparation of the reconciliation of actual amounts on a comparable basis as presented in the SCABAA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the difference noted between the financial statements and the subsidiary ledgers and make the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of the accounts in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant strictly comply with the procedures on the preparation and maintenance of the various forms necessary to support, control and monitor the requisition, receipt and issuance of all inventory items, acquired either through procurement or donation.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant thoroughly review, study and analyze the journal entries made on the affected accounts and prepare the necessary adjusting entries in order to present fairly their balances in the Financial Statements. observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide depreciation for the above noted PPEs, verify and identify the accurate dates of acquisition or when the PPEs were available for use, recompute the depreciation of the PPEs acquired and available for use on or before 15th of the month of acquisition, which were only depreciated on the succeeding months and make the necessary adjusting entries to present fairly the balances of the affected accounts in the financial statements. observations.managementAction: Depreciation was already provided to the Property, Plant and Equipment accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the transactions in their appropriate expense or asset accounts in accordance with the LGU Chart of Accounts to present fairly the relevant accounts in the financial statements. observations.managementAction: Transactions were recorded in their appropriate expense account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant thoroughly review, study and analyze the journal entries made, identify the transactions causing the unreconciled balance and prepare the necessary and appropriate adjusting entries in order to present fairly the balances of the affected accounts in the Financial Statements. Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer make it a practice to reconcile their books of accounts at least quarterly.
observations.managementAction: Reconciled the balances of Statement of Financial Position and Cashbook.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: require the accountable officers/employees to liquidate/settle their long outstanding cash advances in compliance with COA Circular 97-002 and PD 1445. In addition, additional cash advances shall not be granted to any official and employee unless the previous cash advance is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made; and ensure that the request for authority to write-off dormant receivable account to be filed be supported with the required documents as provided in COA Circular 2016-005;
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: In view of the foregoing, and consistent with the provisions of COA Circular 2014-002 and COA Circular 2020-009, we recommended that: Copies of RCD and JEV for the cash donations received for DRRM activities be furnished to the Budget Unit for the latter’s preparation of the Registry of Cash Donations and Utilization; The Municipal Accountant review the journal entries made on the receipt of cash donations and transfer of the unexpended LDRRMF-MOOE for the current year’s appropriation and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to present fairly the balances of the affected accounts in the financial statements; A Cash Donation Utilization Request be prepared by the Budget Unit as support for the charges against the fund; and The Accounting Unit prepare/maintain subsidiary ledger for cash donations received amounting to P100,000.00 and above per donor per purpose while those below P100,000.00 shall be grouped and posted in one SL labeled as various donors per purpose.
observations.managementAction: Management adhered with the recommendation. Copies of RCD and JEV for the cash donations received for DRRM activities be furnished to the Budget Unit for the latter’s preparation of the Registry of Cash Donations and Utilization; The Municipal Accountant review the journal entries made on the receipt of cash donations and transfer of the unexpended LDRRMF-MOOE for the current year’s appropriation and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to present fairly the balances of the affected accounts in the financial statements; A Cash Donation Utilization Request be prepared by the Budget Unit as support for the charges against the fund; and The Accounting Unit prepare/maintain subsidiary ledger for cash donations received amounting to P100,000.00 and above per donor per purpose while those below P100,000.00 shall be grouped and posted in one SL labeled as various donors per purpose.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management ensure that all received and distributed in-kind donations are properly accounted for.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant must observe the proper accounting and recording of transactions in the book of accounts of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: Based on the foregoing, we recommended that the Management submit the lacking documentary requirements on the aforementioned two (2) purchases, which are charged under Bayanihan Grant to Cities and Municipalities. observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee post all the required information in the GPPB Online Portal relating to emergency procurement, if still applicable. Otherwise, henceforth, comply with the reportorial requirement as prescribed by existing rules and regulation to ensure transparency and accountability.
observations.managementAction: Management adhered with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following: The Bids and Awards Committee provide justification on the mode of procurement adopted (whether Shopping or Negotiated Procurement) on the abovementioned purchases of relief goods due to the inconsistencies of mode of procurement indicated between the BAC resolutions and the Report of Fund Utilization for BGCM; For procurement under Negotiated Procurement (Emergency Cases), the Management submit the supporting documents required (such as, Mayor’s or Business Permit, Income Tax Returns or Business Tax Returns and OSS) in Item 4.1 of GPPB Circular No. 01-2020; and The BAC ensure that all procurements of the LGU are properly deliberated to identify the appropriate method of procurement to be adopted and that the details contained in the preparation of related reports are consistent.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management inform the PCSO of the status of the calamity assistance and properly coordinate with them on what appropriate or necessary actions shall be undertaken on the unutilized fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant include in the Disbursement Voucher the Accounting Entries (Box F) portion to provide the entries made and present the gist of the financial transactions, as required in Appendix No. 31, Volume II of Government Accounting Manual for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: In view of the above, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct: The Local Finance Committee to provide justification on the classification of PPAs, made on the monthly Report on Fund Utilization for BGCM as compared to the PPAs reflected in the Supplemental Budget; The Municipal Budget and Municipal Accountant to provide justification on the discrepancy of the amount of disbursements disclosed in the monthly Fund utilization reports as against the actual disbursements (based on DVs) made for each appropriate classification. Moreover, provide justification on the charges made to BGCM for fuel expenses for vehicles charged to BGCM, which were used in the conduct of the regular operations of the LGU; The Municipal Budget to ensure that charges against various funds of the LGU are in accordance with the specific PPAs as incorporated in the approved Budget; and The Management to ensure compliance with applicable guidelines, rules and regulations for the utilization of the LGU funds.
observations.managementAction: No Bayanihan Grant received in CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee, in conformity with Section 12 of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. 9184, for Alternative Modes of Procurement, recommend to the HOPE the award of contract in favor of the supplier or contractor with the Single or Lowest Calculated and Responsive Quotation (for goods or infrastructure projects), or consultant with the Single or Highest Rated and Responsive Proposal (for consulting services). Subsequently, the HoPE or his duly authorized representative shall approve or disapprove the said recommendation.
observations.managementAction: The Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit legal justification for the increase in the Pag-IBIG government share compulsory contribution and ensure strict compliance with the provisions of Rule VI, Section 1 of the IRR of R.A. 9679 or the Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009. Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the difference noted in the amount of government share of Pag-Ibig presented in the payroll with that of the actual amount of remittances.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted legal justification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant henceforth maintain and submit the ledgers of the Other Prepayments and Inter-Agency Payable accounts to support their balances in the Financial Statements. observations.managementAction: Subsidiary Ledgers of the Other Prepayments and Inter-Agency Payable accounts are still not submitted to the Office of the Auditor to support the balances presented in the FS.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain a special account in the General Fund for the operation of the Economic Enterprise of the LGU, through the use of complete subsidiary ledgers, where the receipts, transfers and expenditures shall be taken up properly. 

Moreover, at the end of the year, Trial Balance, Statement of Financial Position, Statement of Financial Performance, and Statement of Cash Flows shall be prepared to assist top management in making financial decisions over the operation of the Economic Enterprise.
observations.managementAction: Financial Statements for the Economic Enterprise were not yet prepared and submitted by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
With the foregoing, we recommended the following: (a) The Inventory Committee to conduct annual physical inventory of all the PPE and Inventories of the Municipality and to properly and completely prepare the corresponding RPCPPE and RPCI and the Management submit such reports on or before January 31; (b) The Municipal Accountant to classify the sub-major PPE accounts to their appropriate major PPE accounts in the detailed Statement of Financial Position based on the provisions of the LGU Chart of accounts; and (c) The Municipal Accountant to reconcile the variances noted on the PPE accounts and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to present fairly the balances in the financial statements.
The Management committed to comply with the recommendations.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant support the bank reconciliations statements with copies of monthly bank statements to facilitate proper review and exert extra effort to ascertain the existence of the Cash in Bank-Time Deposit amounting to P80,406.46, and prepare the necessary adjusting entries, if any.
The Management committed to comply with the recommendations.

We recommended that: (a) the Municipal Accountant reconcile the difference noted between the financial statements and the subsidiary ledger and make the necessary adjusting entry to reflect the correct balance of the Other Receivables account in the FS; (b) the Management ensure that the request for authority to write-off dormant receivable account to be filed should be supported with the required documents as provided in COA Circular 2016-005; and (c) the Management intensify collection efforts for the uncollected receivables.
The Management committed to comply with the recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide complete details of the Accounts Payable in the subsidiary ledgers to determine the existence of long outstanding payables with no claimants for more than 2 years and to ensure that sufficient evidence to support the balance of the Accounts Payable are timely submitted to properly establish the claims recorded the books.
The Management committed to comply with the recommendations.

The Manual requires that Real Property Tax Receivables/Special Education Tax Receivables shall be established at the beginning of the year and not at year-end.
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant a certified list of taxpayers showing the amount due and collectible as of December 31 to be used as basis in setting-up the RPT and SET receivables at the beginning of every year so that reliable year-end balances are presented in the financial statements.
The Management committed to comply with the recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant transfer at year-end the unexpended balances of the QRF and DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liabilities-DRRMF” in the Trust Fund books pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 and Section 21 of Republic Act No. 10121 to present fairly the affected accounts in the Financial Statements. In addition, present properly the available balances of the Mitigation Fund, as to MOOE and CO, in the Notes to FS to determine the correct amount of balance to be transferred to Special Trust Fund and appropriately categorize if programs/projects/activities falls into MOOE or Capital Outlay.
The Management committed to comply with the recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant direct the accountable officers to liquidate/settle their outstanding cash advances in compliance with the regulations of COA Circular 97-002 and PD 1445. In addition, additional cash advance shall not be granted to any officials and employees unless the previous cash advance given is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made.
The Management committed to comply with the recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant study and analyze the general ledgers and journal entries made on these affected accounts and henceforth, prepare adjusting entries, as the case maybe, to present fairly their balances in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile with the Municipal Treasurer the discrepancy noted between the financial statements and the cashbook. Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer make it a practice to reconcile their books of accounts at least quarterly.
The Accounting Office and Municipal Treasury Office agreed to reconcile their records. The Municipal Treasurer commented that daily deposit of collections had been strictly implemented.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the transactions in their appropriate expense accounts in accordance with the LGU Chart of Accounts to present fairly the detailed expenses under the Maintenance and Other Operating expenses in the Statement of Financial Performance. Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer ensure that obligations are charged to proper accounts based on the nature, condition and purpose of the transactions and thereafter, reconcile records with the Accounting Office.
The Management committed to comply with the recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: (1) Record in the books of accounts, the outstanding receivables pertaining to Market Operations to present fairly the balance of receivable and income accounts in the Financial Statements; and (2) Maintain a separate subsidiary ledger per Lessee for the monitoring of collections and reporting of outstanding receivables on a periodic basis.
The Management committed to comply with the recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare a complete set of Financial Statements, supported with special accounts subsidiary ledgers, for the Economic Enterprise to assist the Management in making financial decisions over the operation of the Economic Enterprise.
The Management committed to comply with the recommendations.

We recommended that the MDRRMC prepare the LDRRMFIP by adopting the prescribed format in the Annex A of the COA Circular 2012-002. Likewise, review and update annually the LDRRMFIP to include activities to be funded from the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years, which were transferred to the Special Trust Fund within the next five (5) years.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and MDRRM Officer, henceforth, monitor the unexpended balance of the DRRM Fund in the Special Trust Fund to be utilized within the next five years and any unutilized balance thereafter shall be reverted back to the General Fund available for other social services of the Municipality, in compliance with Item 5.1.13 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
The Management committed to comply with the recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the officer responsible for the accounts, monitor the monthly remittances of the dues and contributions to the GSIS, BIR and HDMF to ensure compliance within the set deadlines.
The Municipal Accountant agreed to monitor the monthly remittance of the dues and contributions of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant monitor the utilization of the financial assistance to ensure that the funds were utilized in accordance with the intended purposes. In addition, the Municipal Account enhance control measures to all fund transfers to other agencies, through proper monitoring and appropriate recognition of receivable and expense accounts in the books.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to issue a check directly in the name of the supplier to stop the practice of granting cash advances to the various Department Heads or other officials for disbursements, which could be practically made through check payable directly in the name of supplier/payee in conformity with Section 93 of PD 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that the date of delivery is properly accomplished upon the preparation of every Purchase Order and the same is duly conformed by the supplier. We recommended that the BAC ensure that the submitted Bid Notice Abstracts for all procurement should be in “ACTIVE” status as proof of its advertisement to the PhilGeps.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant support all that disbursement vouchers with ALOBS or Obligation Request to ensure that the existence of appropriation was legally made for the purpose duly certified by the Budget Officer.

We recommended that: (a) the Municipal Treasurer prepares one check for every single disbursement voucher; and (b) the Municipal Accountant ensures that all DVs and all its supporting documents be marked/stamped “PAID”, upon payment.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following to the Management: (a) To require to the MDRRMC to prepare the LDRRMFIP by adopting the prescribed format in the Annex A of the COA Circular 2012-00 and to review and update annually the LDRRMFIP to include activities to be funded from the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years, which were transferred to the Special Trust Fund; (b) To require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and certify as correct the monthly report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the prescribed format in Annex B of this Circular; and (c) To require the MDRRO to submit to the Auditor the report on or before 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC).

We recommended that the Management consider augmenting the plantilla position of three (3) DRRM staff thru the options provided in Item 5.5 of NDRRMC, DILG, DBM and CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 on the Implementing guidelines for the Establishment of Local DRRM Offices in LGU based on Section 12 of RA 10121. And if the LGU is financially capable and has not exceeded the Personal Services (PS) limitation, the Local Sanggunian, may elevate the organization and staffing of the LDRRM Office.

To enhance further the existing controls of the Agency, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive consider enjoining the concerned department/s to prepare specific written criteria or policies and procedures in the grant of financial assistance to AICS Clients that will fit to the operations of the LGU.

We recommended that the Management strictly observe the procurement of common-use supplies from DBM-PS pursuant to the provisions of 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of Republic Act (RA) No. 9184. In addition, we recommended that the BAC ensure that a Reply/Action Document from the DBM-PS or from its Depot is obtained stating that the requested regular office supplies and equipment that are not available in the Procurement Service.

We recommended that the Management ensure submission of contracts within five (5) days from the date of perfection to the Office of the Auditor for auditorial review in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain a special account in the General Fund for the operation of the Economic Enterprise of the LGU, through the use of complete subsidiary ledgers, where the receipts, transfers and expenditures shall be taken up properly.

Moreover, at the end of the year, Trial Balance, Statement of Financial Position, Statement of Financial Performance, and Statement of Cash Flows shall be prepared to assist top management in making financial decisions over the operation of the Economic Enterprise.

We recommended that the Management, through the Market Supervisor, prepare and update the contracts of the lessees to protect the Municipality from possible loss of income, in case of non-collection of unpaid rental fees, and for the strict implementation by the latter of the applicable provisions of the Code.

We recommended the following to the Management that: (a) the OIC, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant maintain a separate subsidiary ledger per Lessee for the monitoring of collections and reporting of outstanding receivables on a periodic basis; (b) the Municipal Accountant record in the books of accounts, outstanding receivables pertaining to Market Operations to present fairly the balance of receivable accounts in the Financial Statements; and (c) the OIC, Municipal Treasurer ensure compliance with the provisions of the Market Code on the imposition of surcharges and interests for rental fees not paid on time and revocation/cancellation of leased contract with delinquent accounts for non-payment of rental fees for more than three (3) consecutive months.
We recommended that the Market Supervisor and OIC, Municipal Treasurer ensure compliance in the implementation of the rentals rates as stated in their Municipal Market Code.

We recommended that the Management submit the identified lacking documents to support the public bidding conducted in compliance to Item 3.1.2 of COA Circular 2009-01. Moreover, we also recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee ensure compliance with the regulations relevant to the procurement process.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) consider consolidating similar items or projects to be procured upon submission by the different implementing units of their updated/revised PPMPs to refrain from using reimbursement in the procurement of the need requirement of the end – user and to minimize the use of alternative mode of procurement, and to avail of volume discount for bulk purchases.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure the timely submission of the accounts of the Agency. Moreover, the Agency officials concerned should identify the reasons for the delayed submission of accounts and take necessary actions to address the same to ensure compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require and ensure that the inventory committee conduct annual physical inventory of all the PPE of the LGU and to prepare the corresponding required inventory report. The Accounting and the General Service Office should also maintain PPE ledger cards to support the balances in the financial statements.
